sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the company manufactures and include a primary contact CTA.
Product summary should quickly group the main product areas or paper goods without becoming a full catalog.
Production summary must focus on real capabilities, materials or delivery-relevant facts when available.
Trust proof must use honest company facts such as organisation details, references, visible facilities or process competence.
Final CTA should invite contact for quote, product inquiry or business discussion.
Products intro must explain the scope of the offer in concrete terms.
Product list items must describe actual product categories, material variants or use cases, not vague marketing claims.
If pricing is shown, it should be indicative and clearly limited to what the company can honestly state.
Contact CTA should follow the product content and encourage an inquiry tied to needs or volume.
Production intro must connect the company's manufacturing setup to customer value such as consistency, scale or flexibility.
Process steps should be presented in a simple logical order and stay understandable for non-specialist visitors.
Facility overview may include machinery, lines, location or production environment only when information exists in the dossier.
Avoid repeating the same claims already used on the home page unless they are expanded with new specifics.
Sustainability copy must stay factual and avoid unsupported environmental claims.
Materials and sourcing should describe raw materials, recycled content or procurement principles only when known.
Resource and recycling should focus on concrete practices such as waste reduction, recovery, reuse or process efficiency.
CTA should invite follow-up questions about materials, requirements or collaboration rather than repeat generic slogans.
About story must be specific to the company, its background or role in the industry.
Team section should show real people or real functions when available, otherwise be omitted in single-page rendering if unsupported.
Trust proof should prioritise business legitimacy, references and operational credibility over generic adjectives.
Phone, email and address must be present when available and must not use placeholder data.
If opening hours are shown, they should reflect actual office, switchboard or visitor hours.
Map should only be shown when a visitable address or facility location is appropriate to publish.
Contact CTA may include a form and should be suitable for quote requests, purchasing questions or general inquiries.
